response: Hello! I am HailMary, your personal rule-based diagnostic assistant. How can I assist your recovery today?
I can answer 300+ medical FAQs, check your Treatment Progress Score (TPS), fetch your assigned ASHA worker details, review your adherence logs, or instantly trigger emergency workflows. Just ask!
EMERGENCY PROTOCOL INITIATED. Your ASHA worker and local authorities have been alerted.

triggers: who is taking care of me
response: Fetching your assigned ASHA worker details...

Tuberculosis (TB) is a serious infectious bacterial disease that mainly affects the lungs. It is fully curable with a strict 6-9 month medication regimen. If you miss doses, drug-resistant TB can develop.

triggers: what if i miss a dose
what to do if i forget pill
response: If you miss a dose, take it as soon as you remember. If it is already time for your next dose, skip the missed one. Do NOT take a double dose to make up for a missed one. We will log this in your adherence tracker.

triggers: is it normal to feel sick from pills
response: Common TB medication side effects include orange/red urine (due to Rifampin), mild nausea, or fatigue. However, if you experience yellow eyes/skin (jaundice), severe vomiting, or blurred vision, alert your ASHA worker immediately.
A high-protein, calorie-rich diet is critical for TB recovery. Eat lean meats, dal, eggs, and nuts. Absolutely NO ALCOHOL, as it interacts violently with your liver while on TB medications.

triggers: when am i safe to be around others
do i need a mask at home
response: Pulmonary TB is highly contagious before treatment. You must wear a mask and sleep in a separate room for the first 2-3 weeks of treatment. Once your sputum tests are negative, you are no longer contagious.

triggers: how does the app listen to my cough
response: HailMary uses acoustic AI models to detect microscopic changes in your cough frequency and wetness. We compare your current cough against baseline TB audio patterns to calculate an exact Recovery Index.

triggers: what happens when i upload an xray
response: You can upload a photo of your Chest X-Ray. Our Vision AI scans the image for opacities, cavitations, and white patches, dynamically verifying your lung clearing progress against your ASHA records.

The mMRC (Modified Medical Research Council) scale grades your breathlessness from 0 (normal) to 4 (severe). It helps us track if your lung capacity is returning or if you are risking respiratory distress.
Stay safe and remember to log your data! Goodbye!
